hero.title: 5 Days in Kerala.
hero.titleAccent: Kochi, Munnar Tea Hills and the Backwaters.
hero.intro: The Kerala greatest hits, done at a comfortable pace. Fort Kochi's heritage, the rolling green tea carpets of Munnar, and a houseboat night on the backwaters. Five days that capture why everyone falls for God's Own Country.

overview.heading: 5 Days Kerala: Most Important Places: The Full Picture
body: If you have five days for Kerala, this is the route to take. It links the three experiences that define the state: the colonial harbour town of Kochi, the misty tea-covered hills of Munnar, and a night drifting through the Alleppey backwaters on a private houseboat.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, this Kerala package moves you from coast to hills to backwaters with your own vehicle and driver guide. The pacing is relaxed, the scenery constantly changing, and the houseboat night is a highlight you will talk about long after you return home.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Munnar's Tea Hills
items.body: Endless rolling carpets of tea, cool mountain air, waterfalls and viewpoints. Munnar is one of the most beautiful hill stations in South India and a complete change of pace from the coast.

items.title: Alleppey Houseboat Night
items.body: A private houseboat on the backwaters, with fresh Kerala meals cooked onboard and the quiet rhythm of canal life all around you.

items.title: Fort Kochi Heritage
items.body: The Chinese fishing nets, the spice markets, the colonial churches and the art cafes of the old harbour quarter make a characterful introduction to Kerala.

days.title: Arrive Kochi
days.description: Met at Cochin airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the fishing nets.

days.title: Kochi to Munnar
days.description: A scenic drive up to the tea hills of Munnar, past waterfalls and spice gardens.

days.title: Munnar Sightseeing, on to Alleppey
days.description: Tea plantations and viewpoints in Munnar, then descend towards Alleppey.

days.title: Alleppey Houseboat
days.description: Board your private houseboat for an overnight backwater cruise with onboard meals.

days.title: Disembark, Depart
days.description: A final morning cruise, then transfer to Cochin airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: The Best of Kerala, Sequenced Right
whyBook.body: Coast, hills and backwaters in five days takes smart routing to avoid backtracking. We sequence Kochi, Munnar and Alleppey so the journey flows and the scenery keeps changing. Dhesu has run Kerala tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Kerala Most Important Places Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day package is the ideal Kerala itinerary for first-timers who want the essentials: Kochi, Munnar and the backwaters. It captures the coast, the hills and the canals in one smooth private trip.

The drive from the coast up to Munnar's tea hills is spectacular, and the contrast with the backwater houseboat night makes for a varied, memorable trip.

For more, our 6 and 8 day packages add Thekkady's spice plantations and wildlife.

sections.heading: Munnar: Kerala's Tea Country
sections.body:
Munnar sits high in the Western Ghats, surrounded by some of the most beautiful tea plantations in the world. The cool climate, mist and endless green slopes make it a refreshing contrast to coastal Kerala.

Highlights include tea estate visits, the Tea Museum, Mattupetty Dam and viewpoints like Echo Point, all easily covered with your private vehicle.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Cochin International Airport (COK). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this Kerala trip?
items.answer: September to March is ideal, with pleasant weather across the coast, hills and backwaters. Munnar is cool year-round. The monsoon (June to August) brings lush scenery but heavy rain and occasional landslides on hill roads.

items.question: Is Munnar cold?
items.answer: Munnar is cool and pleasant, especially in the mornings and evenings, thanks to its high elevation. A light jacket is useful. It is a refreshing change from the warm, humid coast and one of the trip's highlights.

items.question: How much driving is involved?
items.answer: There are a few scenic drives, notably Kochi to Munnar and Munnar to Alleppey, each a few hours through beautiful country. The private vehicle makes them comfortable, with stops along the way for photos and refreshments.
